asciichat_error_t send_packet_secure (socket_t
  sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void * data, size_t len,
  crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/packet/packet.h>


Send a packet with encryption and compression support.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


type Packet type (from packet_types.h)


data Packet payload data


len Payload data length in bytes


crypto_ctx Cryptographic context for encryption (NULL for
  plaintext)


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Sends a packet with automatic encryption (if crypto context
    provided) and compression (if packet is large enough). Handshake packets are
    never encrypted.




Note


Encryption is applied automatically when crypto_ctx is
  provided and packet type is not a handshake packet.



Compression is applied automatically to large packets based on
    size thresholds and compression ratios.




Handshake packets (packet_is_handshake_type(type) == true) are
    always sent unencrypted, even when crypto_ctx is provided.




Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


type Packet type


data Packet data


len Data length


crypto_ctx Crypto context for encryption


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 430 of file packet.c.


431                                                                    {
432   if (len > MAX_PACKET_SIZE) {
433     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Packet too large: %zu > %d", len, MAX_PACKET_SIZE);
434   }
435 
436   // Handshake packets are ALWAYS sent unencrypted
437   if (packet_is_handshake_type(type)) {
438     return packet_send(sockfd, type, data, len);
439   }
440 
441   // Apply compression if beneficial for large packets
442   const void *final_data = data;
443   size_t final_len = len;
444   void *compressed_data = NULL;
445 
446   // Skip compression for pre-compressed data (Opus audio) or if --no-compress flag is set
447   bool should_skip_compression = packet_is_precompressed(type) || GET_OPTION(no_compress);
448   if (!should_skip_compression && len > COMPRESSION_MIN_SIZE && should_compress(len, len)) {
449     void *temp_compressed = NULL;
450     size_t compressed_size = 0;
451 
452     // Use configured compression level from options (default: 1 for fastest compression)
453     int compression_level = (GET_OPTION(compression_level) > 0) ? GET_OPTION(compression_level) : 1;
454     asciichat_error_t compress_result = compress_data(data, len, &temp_compressed, &compressed_size, compression_level);
455     if (compress_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
456       double ratio = (double)compressed_size / (double)len;
457       if (ratio < COMPRESSION_RATIO_THRESHOLD) {
458         final_data = temp_compressed;
459         final_len = compressed_size;
460         compressed_data = temp_compressed;
461         log_debug("Compressed packet: %zu -> %zu bytes (%.1f%%)", len, compressed_size, ratio * 100.0);
462       } else {
463         SAFE_FREE(temp_compressed);
464       }
465     }
466   }
467 
468   // If no crypto context or crypto not ready, send unencrypted
469   bool ready = crypto_ctx ? crypto_is_ready(crypto_ctx) : false;
470   if (!crypto_ctx || !ready) {
471     log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "CRYPTO_DEBUG: Sending packet type %d UNENCRYPTED (crypto_ctx=%p, ready=%d)", type,
472                    (void *)crypto_ctx, ready);
473     asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(sockfd, type, final_data, final_len);
474     if (compressed_data) {
475       SAFE_FREE(compressed_data);
476     }
477     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
478       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send packet: %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
479     }
480     return result;
481   }
482 
483   // Encrypt the packet: create header + payload, encrypt everything, wrap in PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED
484   packet_header_t header = {.magic = HOST_TO_NET_U64(PACKET_MAGIC),
485                             .type = HOST_TO_NET_U16((uint16_t)type),
486                             .length = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)final_len),
487                             .crc32 = HOST_TO_NET_U32(final_len > 0 ? asciichat_crc32(final_data, final_len) : 0),
488                             .client_id = HOST_TO_NET_U32(0)}; // Always 0 - client_id is not used in practice
489 
490   // Combine header + payload for encryption
491   // Check for integer overflow before addition
492   if (final_len > SIZE_MAX - sizeof(header)) {
493     if (compressed_data) {
494       SAFE_FREE(compressed_data);
495     }
496     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Packet too large: would overflow plaintext buffer size");
497   }
498   size_t plaintext_len = sizeof(header) + final_len;
499   uint8_t *plaintext = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, plaintext_len);
500   if (!plaintext) {
501     if (compressed_data) {
502       SAFE_FREE(compressed_data);
503     }
504     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for plaintext packet");
505   }
506 
507   memcpy(plaintext, &header, sizeof(header));
508   if (final_len > 0 && final_data) {
509     memcpy(plaintext + sizeof(header), final_data, final_len);
510   }
511 
512   // Encrypt
513   // Check for integer overflow before calculating ciphertext size
514   if (plaintext_len > SIZE_MAX - CRYPTO_NONCE_SIZE - CRYPTO_MAC_SIZE) {
515     buffer_pool_free(NULL, plaintext, plaintext_len);
516     if (compressed_data) {
517       SAFE_FREE(compressed_data);
518     }
519     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Packet too large: would overflow ciphertext buffer size");
520   }
521   size_t ciphertext_size = plaintext_len + CRYPTO_NONCE_SIZE + CRYPTO_MAC_SIZE;
522   uint8_t *ciphertext = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, ciphertext_size);
523   if (!ciphertext) {
524     buffer_pool_free(NULL, plaintext, plaintext_len);
525     if (compressed_data) {
526       SAFE_FREE(compressed_data);
527     }
528     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for ciphertext");
529   }
530 
531   size_t ciphertext_len;
532   crypto_result_t result =
533       crypto_encrypt(crypto_ctx, plaintext, plaintext_len, ciphertext, ciphertext_size, &ciphertext_len);
534   buffer_pool_free(NULL, plaintext, plaintext_len);
535 
536   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
537     buffer_pool_free(NULL, ciphertext, ciphertext_size);
538     if (compressed_data) {
539       SAFE_FREE(compressed_data);
540     }
541     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to encrypt packet: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
542   }
543 
544   // Send as PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED
545   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "CRYPTO_DEBUG: Sending encrypted packet (original type %d as PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED)",
546                   type);
547   asciichat_error_t send_result = packet_send(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED, ciphertext, ciphertext_len);
548   buffer_pool_free(NULL, ciphertext, ciphertext_size);
549 
550   if (compressed_data) {
551     SAFE_FREE(compressed_data);
552   }
553 
554   return send_result;
555 }

ssize_t
  send_with_timeout (socket_t sockfd, const void * data, size_t len,
  uint64_t timeout_ns)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/network.h>


Send data with timeout using chunked transmission.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


data Data to send


len Length of data in bytes


timeout_ns Timeout in nanoseconds


Returns


Number of bytes sent on success, -1 on error


Sends data to socket with timeout support. Uses chunked
    transmission for large data to prevent blocking and enable progress
    tracking.




Note


Chunked transmission automatically handles large frames
  by sending in smaller chunks, preventing timeout issues.



Partial sends are handled automatically - function retries until
    all data is sent or timeout occurs.




Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


data Data to send


len Length of data


timeout_seconds Timeout in seconds


Returns


Number of bytes sent, or -1 on error


Definition at line 112 of file
  network/network.c.


112                                                                                               {
113   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
114     errno = EBADF;
115     return -1;
116   }
117 
118   size_t total_sent = 0;
119   const char *data_ptr = (const char *)data;
120 
121   while (total_sent < len) {
122     // Calculate chunk size
123     size_t bytes_to_send = len - total_sent;
124     const size_t MAX_CHUNK_SIZE = 65536; // 64KB chunks for reliable TCP transmission
125     if (bytes_to_send > MAX_CHUNK_SIZE) {
126       bytes_to_send = MAX_CHUNK_SIZE;
127     }
128 
129     // Set up poll for write timeout (in nanoseconds)
130     struct pollfd pfd;
131     pfd.fd = sockfd;
132     pfd.events = POLLOUT;
133     pfd.revents = 0;
134 
135     int64_t effective_timeout_ns = network_is_test_environment() ? (100LL * NS_PER_MS_INT) : (int64_t)timeout_ns;
136     int result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, effective_timeout_ns);
137     if (result <= 0) {
138       if (result == 0) {
139         SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, "send_with_timeout timed out after %llu nanoseconds",
140                       (unsigned long long)timeout_ns);
141         return -1;
142       }
143       if (network_handle_select_error(result)) {
144         continue; // Retry
145       }
146       return -1; // Fatal error
147     }
148 
149     // Check if socket is ready for writing
150     if (!(pfd.revents & POLLOUT)) {
151       SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, "send_with_timeout socket not ready for writing after poll");
152       return -1;
153     }
154 
155     // Use platform-specific send
156     ssize_t sent = socket_send(sockfd, data_ptr + total_sent, bytes_to_send, 0);
157 
158     if (sent < 0) {
159       int error = errno;
160       if (network_handle_send_error(error)) {
161         continue; // Retry
162       }
163       return -1; // Fatal error
164     }
165 
166     if (sent > 0) {
167       total_sent += (size_t)sent;
168     }
169   }
170 
171   return (ssize_t)total_sent;
172 }

    network_is_test_environment, NS_PER_MS_INT,
    SET_ERRNO_SYS, socket_poll(), and socket_send().

asciichat_error_t set_socket_timeout (socket_t
  sockfd, uint64_t timeout_ns)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/network.h>


Set socket timeout for send/receive operations.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


timeout_ns Timeout in nanoseconds (0 to disable timeout)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Configures socket-level timeout for send and receive operations.
    This sets SO_SNDTIMEO and SO_RCVTIMEO socket options.




Note


Socket-level timeouts work in addition to
  application-level timeouts in send_with_timeout() and
  recv_with_timeout().


Set socket timeout for send/receive operations.




Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


timeout_ns Timeout in nanoseconds (converted to milliseconds for socket
  level)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Sets socket-level timeouts (SO_RCVTIMEO/SO_SNDTIMEO) as a safety
    net fallback. Actual precision depends on platform: milliseconds on most
    systems, microseconds on some. Works in conjunction with application-level
    timeouts via socket_poll.


Definition at line 321 of file
  network/network.c.


321                                                                            {
322   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
323     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket file descriptor");
324   }
325 
326   // Convert nanoseconds to milliseconds for socket-level timeout
327   // Note: socket-level timeouts have ~millisecond granularity on most platforms
328   uint64_t timeout_ms = timeout_ns / (1000 * 1000);
329   if (timeout_ms == 0 && timeout_ns > 0) {
330     timeout_ms = 1; // Ensure at least 1ms for non-zero timeouts
331   }
332 
333   // Set both receive and send timeouts using struct timeval
334   struct timeval tv;
335   tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(timeout_ms / 1000);
336   tv.tv_usec = (long)((timeout_ms % 1000) * 1000);
337 
338   // Set receive timeout
339   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_RCVTIMEO, &tv, sizeof(tv)) != 0) {
340     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to set socket receive timeout");
341   }
342 
343   // Set send timeout
344   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_SNDTIMEO, &tv, sizeof(tv)) != 0) {
345     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to set socket send timeout");
346   }
347 
348   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
349 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, SET_ERRNO,
    SET_ERRNO_SYS, and socket_setsockopt().


Referenced by add_client().






asciichat_error_t socket_configure_buffers
  (socket_t sockfd)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/network.h>


Configure socket buffers and TCP_NODELAY for optimal
  performance.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or ERROR_NETWORK if all options
  fail


Configures socket with large send/receive buffers (1MB each) and
    enables TCP_NODELAY for low-latency real-time video streaming.




CONFIGURATION:


    
  
  • Send buffer: 1MB (SO_SNDBUF)
    • 
  
  • Receive buffer: 1MB (SO_RCVBUF)
    • 
  
  • TCP_NODELAY: enabled (disables Nagle's algorithm)
    • 



BEHAVIOR: Attempts to set all three socket options independently.
    Individual failures are logged as warnings but do not prevent attempting
    other options. This ensures TCP_NODELAY is always set even if buffer size
    configuration fails (common on systems with strict limits). Only returns
    error if ALL options fail.




Note


Large buffers prevent packet loss during frame bursts



TCP_NODELAY ensures frames are sent immediately without
  buffering




Call this immediately after socket creation/connection




Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_NETWORK_CONFIG on
  failure


Definition at line 388 of file
  network/network.c.


388                                                             {
389   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
390     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid socket file descriptor");
391   }
392 
393   int failed_options = 0;
394 
395   // Attempt to configure 1MB send buffer for optimal frame transmission
396   int send_buffer_size = 1024 * 1024;
397   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_SNDBUF, &send_buffer_size, sizeof(send_buffer_size)) < 0) {
398     log_warn("Failed to set send buffer size to 1MB: %s", network_error_string());
399     failed_options++;
400   }
401 
402   // Attempt to configure 1MB receive buffer for optimal frame reception
403   int recv_buffer_size = 1024 * 1024;
404   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_RCVBUF, &recv_buffer_size, sizeof(recv_buffer_size)) < 0) {
405     log_warn("Failed to set receive buffer size to 1MB: %s", network_error_string());
406     failed_options++;
407   }
408 
409   // Attempt to enable TCP_NODELAY to disable Nagle's algorithm for low-latency transmission
410   // This must be set even if buffer configuration fails, as it's essential for real-time video.
411   int tcp_nodelay = 1;
412   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_NODELAY, &tcp_nodelay, sizeof(tcp_nodelay)) < 0) {
413     log_warn("Failed to set TCP_NODELAY: %s", network_error_string());
414     failed_options++;
415   }
416 
417   // Return error only if ALL options failed
418   if (failed_options >= 3) {
419     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to configure all socket options");
420   }
421 
422   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
423 }

    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_warn, network_error_string(),
    SET_ERRNO_SYS, and socket_setsockopt().

416     }
417     break;
418 
419   case INSTALL_METHOD_GITHUB:
420   case INSTALL_METHOD_UNKNOWN:
421   default:
422     snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "https://github.com/zfogg/ascii-chat/releases/tag/%s",
423              latest_version ? latest_version : "latest");
424     break;
425   }

asciichat_error_t update_check_load_cache
  (update_check_result_t * result)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/update_checker.h>


Load cached update check result. Reads cache file from
    ~/.config/ascii-chat/last_update_check. Cache format (3 lines):


    
  
  • Line 1: Unix timestamp
    • 
  
  • Line 2: Latest version tag (or empty if check failed)
    • 
  
  • Line 3: Latest SHA (full 40 chars, or empty if check failed)
    • 



Parameters


result Output structure with cached results


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if cache valid, error if
  missing/corrupt


Definition at line 60 of file update_checker.c.


60                                                                          {
61   if (!result) {
62     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "NULL result pointer");
63   }
64 
65   memset(result, 0, sizeof(*result));
66 
67   char *cache_path = get_cache_file_path();
68   if (!cache_path) {
69     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION, "Could not determine cache file path");
70   }
71 
72   FILE *f = platform_fopen("file_stream", cache_path, "r");
73   if (!f) {
74     const char *error_msg = file_read_error_message(cache_path);
75     SAFE_FREE(cache_path);
76     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION, "%s", error_msg);
77   }
78 
79   // Read line 1: timestamp
80   char line[512];
81   if (!fgets(line, sizeof(line), f)) {
82     fclose(f);
83     SAFE_FREE(cache_path);
84     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION, "Failed to read timestamp from cache");
85   }
86   result->last_check_time = (time_t)atoll(line);
87 
88   // Read line 2: latest version (may be empty if check failed)
89   if (fgets(line, sizeof(line), f)) {
90     // Remove newline
91     size_t len = strlen(line);
92     if (len > 0 && line[len - 1] == '\n') {
93       line[len - 1] = '\0';
94     }
95     SAFE_STRNCPY(result->latest_version, line, sizeof(result->latest_version));
96   }
97 
98   // Read line 3: latest SHA (may be empty if check failed)
99   if (fgets(line, sizeof(line), f)) {
100     // Remove newline
101     size_t len = strlen(line);
102     if (len > 0 && line[len - 1] == '\n') {
103       line[len - 1] = '\0';
104     }
105     SAFE_STRNCPY(result->latest_sha, line, sizeof(result->latest_sha));
106   }
107 
108   fclose(f);
109 
110   // Fill in current version/SHA
111   SAFE_STRNCPY(result->current_version, ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_STRING, sizeof(result->current_version));
112   SAFE_STRNCPY(result->current_sha, ASCII_CHAT_GIT_COMMIT_HASH, sizeof(result->current_sha));
113 
114   // Determine if update is available using version comparison (if we have cached data)
115   if (result->latest_version[0] != '\0') {
116     semantic_version_t current_ver = version_parse(result->current_version);
117     semantic_version_t latest_ver = version_parse(result->latest_version);
118 
119     if (current_ver.valid && latest_ver.valid) {
120       int cmp = version_compare(latest_ver, current_ver);
121       result->update_available = (cmp > 0); // Update available if latest > current
122       result->check_succeeded = true;
123     } else {
124       // Cache contains invalid version data - delete it
125       log_warn("Update cache contains invalid version data (current:%s, latest:%s) - deleting corrupted cache",
126                result->current_version, result->latest_version);
127       if (remove(cache_path) != 0) {
128         log_warn("Failed to delete corrupted cache file: %s", cache_path);
129       }
130       SAFE_FREE(cache_path);
131       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_FORMAT, "Corrupted cache file deleted");
132     }
133   }
134 
135   SAFE_FREE(cache_path);
136   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
137 }

    ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_STRING, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    update_check_result_t::check_succeeded,

    update_check_result_t::current_version, ERROR_FILE_OPERATION,

asciichat_error_t update_check_perform
  (update_check_result_t * result)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/update_checker.h>


Check for updates from GitHub releases API. Performs DNS
    connectivity test, fetches latest release from GitHub API, compares SHA with
    current build, and caches the result.




Parameters


result Output structure with check results


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success (check completed), error
  otherwise


Note


On timeout or network error, logs warning and marks as
  checked in cache



On DNS failure, does not update cache (will retry when online)




Timeout is 2 seconds for the entire operation




Definition at line 267 of file update_checker.c.


267                                                                       {
268   if (!result) {
269     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "NULL result pointer");
270   }
271 
272   memset(result, 0, sizeof(*result));
273 
274   // Fill in current version and SHA
275   SAFE_STRNCPY(result->current_version, ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_STRING, sizeof(result->current_version));
276   SAFE_STRNCPY(result->current_sha, ASCII_CHAT_GIT_COMMIT_HASH, sizeof(result->current_sha));
277   result->last_check_time = time(NULL);
278 
279   // Test DNS connectivity first
280   if (!dns_test_connectivity(GITHUB_API_HOSTNAME)) {
281     log_warn("No internet connectivity detected, skipping update check");
282     // Don't update cache - we'll retry when online
283     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "DNS connectivity test failed");
284   }
285 
286   // Fetch latest release from GitHub API
287   log_info("Checking for updates from GitHub releases...");
288   char *response = https_get(GITHUB_API_HOSTNAME, GITHUB_RELEASES_PATH);
289   if (!response) {
290     log_warn("Failed to fetch GitHub releases API (timeout or network error)");
291     // Mark as checked even though it failed (prevents repeated offline attempts)
292     result->check_succeeded = false;
293     update_check_save_cache(result);
294     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to fetch GitHub releases");
295   }
296 
297   // Parse JSON response
298   char latest_tag[64] = {0};
299   char latest_sha[41] = {0};
300   char release_url[512] = {0};
301 
302   if (!parse_github_release_json(response, latest_tag, sizeof(latest_tag), latest_sha, sizeof(latest_sha), release_url,
303                                  sizeof(release_url))) {
304     log_error("Failed to parse GitHub API response");
305     SAFE_FREE(response);
306     result->check_succeeded = false;
307     update_check_save_cache(result);
308     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_FORMAT, "Failed to parse GitHub API JSON");
309   }
310 
311   SAFE_FREE(response);
312 
313   // Fill in result
314   SAFE_STRNCPY(result->latest_version, latest_tag, sizeof(result->latest_version));
315   SAFE_STRNCPY(result->latest_sha, latest_sha, sizeof(result->latest_sha));
316   SAFE_STRNCPY(result->release_url, release_url, sizeof(result->release_url));
317   result->check_succeeded = true;
318 
319   // Compare versions semantically
320   semantic_version_t current_ver = version_parse(result->current_version);
321   semantic_version_t latest_ver = version_parse(result->latest_version);
322 
323   if (!current_ver.valid || !latest_ver.valid) {
324     log_warn("Failed to parse version strings for comparison (current: %s, latest: %s)", result->current_version,
325              result->latest_version);
326     result->update_available = false;
327   } else {
328     int cmp = version_compare(latest_ver, current_ver);
329     result->update_available = (cmp > 0); // Update available if latest > current
330   }
331 
332   if (result->update_available) {
333     log_info("Update available: %s (%.*s) → %s (%.*s)", result->current_version, 8, result->current_sha,
334              result->latest_version, 8, result->latest_sha);
335   } else {
336     log_info("Already on latest version: %s (%.*s)", result->current_version, 8, result->current_sha);
337   }
338 
339   // Save to cache
340   update_check_save_cache(result);
341 
342   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
343 }

    update_check_result_t::current_version,

asciichat_error_t update_check_startup
  (update_check_result_t * result)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/update_checker.h>


Perform startup update check with caching. Designed for automatic
    startup checks. Uses cache if fresh (< 7 days), otherwise performs fresh
    check. Non-blocking and silent on failure.




Parameters


result Output structure with check results (can be
  NULL if you only want logging)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if check succeeded, error otherwise


Note


Does nothing if cache is fresh



Logs warning on network failure but does not fail




Updates cache only if check succeeds




Definition at line 444 of file update_checker.c.


444                                                                       {
445   update_check_result_t local_result;
446   update_check_result_t *target = result ? result : &local_result;
447 
448   // Try to load from cache first
449   asciichat_error_t cache_err = update_check_load_cache(target);
450   if (cache_err == ASCIICHAT_OK && update_check_is_cache_fresh(target)) {
451     // Cache is fresh, use it
452     log_debug("Using cached update check result (age: %.1f days)",
453               (time(NULL) - target->last_check_time) / (double)SEC_PER_DAY);
454     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
455   }
456 
457   // Cache is stale or missing, perform fresh check
458   log_debug("Performing automatic update check (cache %s)", cache_err == ASCIICHAT_OK ? "stale" : "missing");
459   asciichat_error_t check_err = update_check_perform(target);
460   if (check_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
461     // Check failed, but don't fail startup
462     log_debug("Automatic update check failed (continuing startup)");
463     return check_err;
464   }
465 
466   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
467 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    update_check_result_t::last_check_time, log_debug,
    SEC_PER_DAY, update_check_is_cache_fresh(),
    update_check_load_cache(), and update_check_perform().
